name: Dubois-Pike
description_short: Dubois-Pike Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, nonprofit credit union organized in 1971, offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards to Indiana members.
description_long:
Dubois-Pike Federal Credit Union was organized in 1971 and operates as a member-owned, nonprofit financial institution regulated by the National Credit Union Administration under a federal charter. Unlike traditional banks, the credit union returns profits to members rather than shareholders, which directly benefits account holders through higher dividend rates on savings and lower interest rates on loans. The organization is headquartered in Jasper, Indiana, and serves its membership community with a focus on trust and member-centric financial solutions.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products including digital banking services, multiple savings account types (checking, savings, club accounts, money market, share certificates, and IRAs), consumer lending (auto loans, personal loans, credit cards, mortgages, home equity loans, HELOCs, student loans, and recreational vehicle loans), and protective services (credit life and disability insurance, GAP insurance, vehicle coverage). They provide ATM and VISA debit card access, skip-a-payment options, courtesy pay overdraft protection, and pre-paid gift and travel cards. Members can access accounts via mobile app and online banking platforms.

Dubois-Pike distinguishes itself through genuine member governance—each member aged 16 or older votes on the Board of Directors at annual meetings, and both the Board and Supervisory Committee serve without compensation. The organization emphasizes financial education, offers scholarships including the Jim Schroeder Memorial Scholarship for Educators, and publishes regular newsletters to keep members informed. Their stated mission focuses on trust and innovative solutions, with a vision centered on member trust through service.

As a regional credit union, Dubois-Pike is best suited for Indiana residents seeking traditional banking services with the member-ownership advantage. However, the credit union's geographic and membership-based limitations mean potential applicants should verify eligibility requirements. Limited information on their website regarding specific rates, fees, membership eligibility criteria, and service coverage area may require direct contact with the institution.
services: VISA debit cards and ATM access
pros: Member-owned nonprofit structure returns profits to members through higher savings dividends and lower loan rates
Comprehensive product suite including auto loans, mortgages, personal loans, credit cards, and investment products
Democratic governance with member voting rights and volunteer board leadership
Federal charter and NCUA regulation providing standard credit union protections
Mobile app and online banking for account management from anywhere
Scholarship programs including educator-focused Jim Schroeder Memorial Scholarship
Multiple account protection features including credit life/disability insurance and GAP insurance
cons: Limited geographic footprint and membership eligibility requirements not clearly detailed on website
No transparent fee schedule or current rate information displayed on main pages
Minimal information about specific membership eligibility criteria or field of membership
No evidence of 24/7 customer support; only phone number listed (812-634-9278)
Limited online account opening information; membership process unclear from website content
best_for: Indiana residents seeking member-owned banking with lower rates and higher dividends
Individuals eligible for membership who want democratic control over financial institution governance
Consumers needing comprehensive lending services from auto loans to mortgages within one institution
diagnosis: Dubois-Pike Federal Credit Union is best for Indiana-based consumers seeking traditional banking services with the member-ownership advantage of lower fees and higher returns. Primary caveat: membership eligibility and geographic service limitations are not clearly detailed online, requiring direct contact to confirm eligibility and access before opening an account.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
